channel.title: Daily Feed (Updated 9 AM BD)
channel.description: Daily Feed (Updated 9 AM BD) generated by script

item.title: UN Rights Group Demands That Equatorial Guinea Free an Activist Held in a Dungeon
item.description: Recently, the United Nations Working Group on Arbitrary Detention published its opinion, and the message is clear. The Obiang regime of Equatorial Guinea is holding a lawyer and activist in the country, Anacleto Micha Ndong Nlang, in arbitrary detention and it must release him immediately. In plain terms, the Human Rights Council, of which the ... Read more

item.title: Ebola outbreak in DR Congo collides with conflict and hunger, WHO warns
item.description: The UN World Health Organization (WHO) on Wednesday warned that eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo faces a “catastrophic collision of disease and conflict” as a fast-spreading Ebola outbreak outpaces containment efforts in a region already battered by armed violence, mass displacement and acute hunger.

item.title: How Europe’s Waste Could Supply Over Half of Critical Material Demand – Report
item.description: Europe’s growing mountain of waste could become one of its most important sources of critical raw materials, according to a major new report that warns of rising geopolitical risks and growing global competition for minerals needed in the green and digital economy. The report, released by the Horizon Europe-funded FutuRaM project, says Europe’s “urban mine” […]

item.title: US Blocks VIPs From Iran and Russia From Participating in Security Council Debate
item.description: The United States denied visas to senior government officials from Russia and Iran, respectively, that would have enabled them to take part in person at a high-level United Nations Security Council meeting on international peace and security. The refusal cast a shadow over a debate convened by China to examine the erosion of the very ... Read more
